name: True Forex Funds
description_short: True Forex Funds offers funded forex trading accounts up to $200K via a 2-step evaluation with no time limits and up to 80% profit splits.
description_long:
True Forex Funds (TFF) is a proprietary trading firm that provides retail traders with access to funded accounts ranging from $10,000 to $200,000. Through a structured two-phase evaluation challenge, traders prove their consistency and discipline before receiving a live funded account to trade on behalf of the firm.

The evaluation consists of Phase 1 (8% profit target) and Phase 2 (5% profit target), both enforced with a 5% daily drawdown limit and 10% maximum drawdown limit. Critically, there are no minimum or maximum trading day requirements in either phase, allowing traders to work at their own pace without artificial time pressure. Once funded, traders retain up to 80% of all profits generated.

TFF supports MetaTrader 4 and MetaTrader 5 platforms, giving access to major and minor forex pairs, global indices, commodities, and metals. Expert Advisors and copy trading are permitted, making the firm a viable option for algorithmic and automated traders. A notable feature is the challenge fee refund policy—the initial evaluation fee is credited back after a trader receives their first funded account payout.

True Forex Funds entered the prop firm market around 2022 and has steadily built a following among forex-focused traders. While it lacks the brand recognition and long track record of industry leaders like FTMO, it competes on value, flexible rules, and a straightforward payout structure. The firm is best suited for experienced forex traders seeking capital access without risking significant personal funds.
pros: No time limits on either evaluation phase
Challenge fee refunded upon first funded account payout
Expert Advisors and automated trading permitted
Simple and transparent two-phase rule structure
cons: Newer firm with shorter track record than top-tier competitors
Limited educational resources for developing traders
No free trial or demo evaluation available
Customer support can be slow during high-volume periods
best_for: experienced traders wanting flexible evaluation rules
